% Auto-generated by recursive_marker_transformer.mor_tables -- do not edit.
\paragraph{How few tokens suffice.} Token reduction. SMART keeps only $M$ interpretable tokens, so attention costs $O(M^2)$ rather than $O(N^2)$ in the number of genes. Accuracy is reported as $M$ shrinks; a few dozen to a few hundred tokens recover most of the full-gene signal..
\begin{table*}[t]\centering\footnotesize
\setlength{\tabcolsep}{5pt}
\caption{Macro-F1 as the number of marker tokens $M$ is reduced (token reduction).}
\label{tab:exp-tokens}
\resizebox{\ifdim\width>\textwidth\textwidth\else\width\fi}{!}{%
\begin{tabular}{lrrrrr}
\toprule
Dataset / #tokens M & M=16 & M=32 & M=64 & M=128 & M=256 \\
\midrule
tabula\_muris & 36.3 & 53.2 & 65.2 & 70.9 & 76.8 \\
pancreas & 36.1 & 50.3 & 54.0 & 59.0 & 57.2 \\
common\_class & 14.7 & 30.9 & 33.9 & 64.8 & 74.9 \\
prototype & 63.2 & 79.1 & 87.4 & 93.8 & 97.5 \\
baron & 28.1 & 43.3 & 42.1 & 61.9 & 72.0 \\
segerstolpe & 5.1 & 6.1 & 10.0 & 4.5 & 7.5 \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
}
\end{table*}
\paragraph{Recursion versus independent layers across model sizes.} Recursion vs independent depth across model sizes: independent layers (Vanilla), one weight-shared block applied $K$ times (Recursive), and adaptive routing (SMART). Recursive matches independent at a fraction of the parameters..
\begin{table*}[t]\centering\footnotesize
\setlength{\tabcolsep}{5pt}
\caption{Macro-F1 of independent layers (Vanilla), one weight-shared block (Recursive), and adaptive routing (SMART).}
\label{tab:exp-scaling}

\begin{tabular}{lrrrrrr}

Arch x size (macro\_f1) & tabula\_muris & pancreas & common\_class & prototype & baron & segerstolpe \\
\midrule
Vanilla (independent) d=48 & 74.5 & 51.1 & 78.8 & 95.5 & 69.8 & 7.3 \\
Vanilla (independent) d=96 & 78.0 & 55.5 & 71.7 & 94.7 & 64.5 & 9.8 \\
Vanilla (independent) d=192 & 78.0 & 56.8 & 3.0 & 96.2 & 3.3 & 9.2 \\
Vanilla (independent) d=384 & 52.4 & 5.3 & 3.0 & 76.3 & 3.3 & 5.9 \\
Recursive (shared) d=48 & 73.7 & 54.3 & 77.6 & 94.3 & 67.0 & 6.6 \\

Recursive (shared) d=192 & 79.7 & 61.5 & 62.1 & 95.2 & 59.7 & 7.0 \\
Recursive (shared) d=384 & 0.5 & 53.8 & 23.0 & 74.2 & 16.4 & 6.4 \\
MoR (SMART) d=48 & 68.7 & 57.8 & 75.6 & 93.5 & 67.6 & 4.8 \\

\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
}
\end{table*}
\paragraph{Weight-sharing schemes.} Between fully shared and fully independent lie graded sharing schemes -- Cycle, Sequence, Middle-Cycle and Middle-Sequence -- trading parameters for capacity..
\begin{table*}[t]\centering\footnotesize
\setlength{\tabcolsep}{5pt}
\caption{Cycle, Sequence, Middle-Cycle and Middle-Sequence sharing between the fully-shared and fully-independent extremes.}
\label{tab:exp-sharing}

\begin{tabular}{lrrrrrrrrrr}
\toprule
Sharing scheme (K=6) & tabula\_muris & pancreas & common\_class & prototype & baron & segerstolpe & prostate & blca & stad & panmeta\_subtype \\
\midrule
shared (1 block) & 75.1 & 61.0 & 68.9 & 93.6 & 60.2 & 3.4 & 72.3 & 42.7 & 38.8 & -- \\
Cycle (3) & 80.5 & 54.4 & 71.6 & 95.2 & 70.5 & 6.9 & 57.1 & 40.4 & -- & -- \\
Sequence (3) & 78.5 & 57.6 & 0.8 & 93.9 & 66.1 & 4.5 & 59.5 & 43.8 & -- & -- \\
Middle-Cycle (3) & 80.1 & 56.4 & 67.0 & 94.9 & 61.0 & 7.8 & 60.5 & 40.4 & -- & -- \\
Middle-Sequence (3) & 80.1 & 56.4 & 67.0 & 94.9 & 61.0 & 7.8 & 60.5 & 40.4 & -- & -- \\
independent (6) & 79.9 & 57.0 & 3.0 & 95.4 & 65.3 & 7.2 & 59.6 & 44.4 & 56.2 & -- \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
}
\end{table*}
\paragraph{Where computation is spent.} An interpretability view of the adaptive loop: the mean recursion depth assigned to each marker token and the fraction of tokens still active at each recursion step..
\begin{table*}[t]\centering\footnotesize

pancreas & 2.75/4 & 1.00, 0.75, 0.50, 0.50 \\
common\_class & 2.75/4 & 1.00, 0.75, 0.50, 0.50 \\
prototype & 2.75/4 & 1.00, 0.75, 0.50, 0.50 \\
baron & 2.75/4 & 1.00, 0.75, 0.50, 0.50 \\
segerstolpe & 2.75/4 & 1.00, 0.75, 0.50, 0.50 \\

\paragraph{Key/value reuse across recursions.} Whether the first recursion's attention keys/values can be reused across later steps (a cache) instead of recomputed, trading a little accuracy for compute..
\begin{table*}[t]\centering\footnotesize
\setlength{\tabcolsep}{5pt}
\caption{Recomputing vs reusing the first-step attention keys/values across recursion steps.}
\label{tab:exp-cache}
\resizebox{\ifdim\width>\textwidth\textwidth\else\width\fi}{!}{%
\begin{tabular}{lrrrrrrrrrr}
\toprule
KV strategy (macro-F1) & tabula\_muris & pancreas & common\_class & prototype & baron & segerstolpe & prostate & blca & stad & panmeta\_subtype \\
\midrule
recompute K/V (no cache) & 79.9 & 54.1 & 70.4 & 93.9 & 65.6 & 8.6 & 74.7 & 43.3 & 36.4 & -- \\
reuse step-1 K/V (step-cache) & 71.6 & 58.5 & 71.8 & 93.2 & 59.6 & 6.4 & 82.8 & 39.6 & 38.8 & -- \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
}
\end{table*}
\paragraph{Warm-starting recursion from a fixed-depth model.} Whether a recursive model benefits from initialising its shared block from a trained fixed-depth model and continuing training, versus training from scratch..
\begin{table*}[t]\centering\footnotesize
\setlength{\tabcolsep}{5pt}
\caption{Initialising the shared block from a trained fixed-depth model vs training from scratch.}

\begin{tabular}{lrrrrrr}

Warm-start (macro-F1) & tabula\_muris & pancreas & common\_class & prototype & baron & segerstolpe \\
\midrule
fixed-depth source & 75.2 & 51.8 & 69.0 & 92.9 & 68.1 & 8.3 \\
MoR from scratch & 71.7 & 62.8 & 65.8 & 95.5 & 61.5 & 5.4 \\
MoR warm-started & 65.8 & 61.6 & 64.8 & 93.3 & 55.8 & 6.9 \\
warm-start gain & -5.9 & -1.2 & -0.9 & -2.2 & -5.6 & +1.5 \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
}
\end{table*}
